title: Define the explicit transaction state machine contract
summary: Define the Phase 1 contract for v0.4.13 that makes transaction-aware task management explicit as a state machine. The contract must define valid and forbidden transaction transitions, separate new-start from resume behavior, and clarify the synchronization guarantees required between canonical transaction events and materialized transaction state so that a single ticket is managed as one coherent transaction from start to terminal completion.
outputs: Documented explicit transaction state machine for ticket execution from begin through terminal completion
Defined valid transitions, forbidden transitions, and integrity-failure boundaries for transaction-aware task management
Defined canonical start, resume, verify, commit, end, and synchronization semantics to guide implementation and regression protection
acceptance_criteria: The Phase 1 contract explicitly defines the transaction lifecycle states and sub-states used for transaction-aware task management.
The contract explicitly distinguishes new transaction start from resume and states that resume must not emit a second tx.begin for a non-terminal transaction.
The contract defines the valid happy-path, blocked-path, verify-retry, and resume flows for a single ticket transaction.

The contract defines forbidden flows including lifecycle before tx.begin, duplicate non-terminal tx.begin, verify result without verify start, commit start without verify pass, event/state synchronization drift, active transaction loss, and post-terminal follow-up events.
The contract states that canonical event progression and materialized transaction state must remain synchronized and that divergence is an integrity failure rather than a recoverable success path.
The contract defines the required behavior for begin semantics, resume semantics, verify semantics, commit semantics, and terminal end semantics clearly enough to guide implementation tickets.
tasks: Review the v0.4.13 draft and the observed workflow failures to identify the missing state-machine contract details.
Define the top-level transaction lifecycle states, verify and commit sub-states, and the canonical meaning of each state.
Define valid transition sequences for happy path, blocked path, verification retry, and resume behavior.
Define forbidden transitions and explicit integrity-failure conditions for transaction-invalid flows.
Define the synchronization contract between canonical transaction events, active transaction materialization, last_applied_seq, verify state, commit state, and guarded workflow operations.
Document the Phase 1 contract so that later implementation tickets can use one consistent model for runtime enforcement and regression testing.
notes: This is a contract-definition ticket for Phase 1, not the implementation ticket.
Do not weaken strict workflow guards or transaction validation rules as part of this ticket.
Keep the contract explicit enough that all runtime helpers can share one transaction model instead of reconstructing behavior from distributed assumptions.
Use the observed failures as concrete examples of specification-violating flows that the contract must classify and forbid.
The contract should support deterministic resumability by requiring that one ticket remains one coherent transaction lineage until it reaches a terminal outcome.
